Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For Default Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-05-2023 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar May-16-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JESSICA L. SHAW, Esq. of Tierney Law Group, PC (Tel: 925-362-3364) for plaintiff;
Atty. CATHERINE M. ADAMS, Esq. of WFBM, LLP (Tel: 415-781-7072) for defendants S&C Construction, LLC and Sean Patrick Carroll.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS SEAN CARROLL, S&C CONSTRUCTION, LLC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' "motion to set aside default judgment" is granted.

A strong policy of California's courts is to adjudicate cases on their merits. The trial court has broad discretion to vacate a default judgment. (Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2006) 146 Cal.App.4th 488, 495.) And "it is now well-acknowledged that an attorney has an ethical obligation to warn opposing counsel that the attorney is about to take an adversary's default." (Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 135 [emphasis original].)

Defendants demonstrate confusion as to whether they had legal counsel, misunderstanding of legal proceedings and pandemic-related stress, and thus "mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect." (Carroll Dec. 2:5-3:9; CCP 473.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

Off Calendar Mar-30-2023 Continued To Jun-14-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JESSICA L. SHAW, Esq. of Tierney Law Group, PC (Tel: 925-362-3364) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SEAN CARROLL'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Sean Carroll's "motion to set aside entry default" is denied without prejudice for failure to include an answer. (CCP 473(b).)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
